rationale: In the literature review, Gal and McShane (2012) is used to support specific claims about what subgoal success can do (including increased goal commitment) rather than only as a generic background citation. The citing paper also draws on that debt-management finding when motivating its own credit-card repayment application in Study 10. While it does not reuse the cited paper's methods or data, the citation meaningfully informs the paper's framing and empirical positioning around post-subgoal behavior.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Gal and McShane (2012) as prior empirical grounding for subgoal success effects in debt contexts, and uses it to motivate its own theorizing and debt-repayment test setting. This supports the conceptual foundation on which the PLRM-based moderation and intervention analyses are built.
